name.fr: Pain à la drêche de bière

description.en: Damp, spent barley grain left over from brewing gets folded into a soft, enriched loaf with milk, apple juice and molasses. A modern brewery-and-bakery pairing that turns a byproduct breweries discard by the truckload into a malty, toasty everyday bread.
description.de: Feuchter Biertreber, das Nebenprodukt des Brauens, wird in einen weichen, angereicherten Teig mit Milch, Apfelsaft und Melasse eingearbeitet. Eine moderne Brauerei-Bäckerei-Partnerschaft, die ein sonst tonnenweise entsorgtes Nebenprodukt zu malzigem Alltagsbrot macht.
description.es: El bagazo de cerveza húmedo, sobrante de la elaboración, se incorpora a una masa suave y enriquecida con leche, jugo de manzana y melaza. Una alianza moderna entre cervecería y panadería que convierte un subproducto desechado a toneladas en un pan cotidiano, maltoso y tostado.
description.fr: La drêche humide, résidu du brassage de la bière, s'incorpore à une pâte tendre et enrichie de lait, de jus de pomme et de mélasse. Un mariage moderne brasserie-boulangerie qui transforme un sous-produit jeté par tonnes en un pain quotidien, malté et grillé.

origin.note: Spent grain is beer brewing's biggest byproduct by volume; pairing a brewery with a bakery to bake it into bread is a 21st-century sustainability practice, not a historical recipe.

yield.shape: free-standing loaf on a sheet pan

keeping.note: A moist, tender loaf from all the added liquid; a good way to use spent grain a brewery would otherwise discard.

summary.en: Dissolve the yeast in warm water.
Heat butter with milk until melted, then stir in apple juice, molasses, salt and sugar.
Mix the yeast mixture into the warm liquids, then work in the drained spent grain and whole wheat flour.
Gradually knead in the white flour until the dough is smooth and workable.
Deflate, divide in two, and shape into free-standing loaves on a cornmeal-dusted, parchment-lined sheet pan.
Brush with egg wash, scatter a little spent grain on top, and let rise again.
Bake at about 165°C for approximately 45 minutes until deep golden and hollow-sounding.

source.adaptation: Cup measurements converted to grams; spent grain and flour weights are estimates since densities vary by brew and mill. Hydration counts only water, milk and juice at their approximate water content.
